rationale: Report omits mandatory Sampling Notice.
Large generated tree should be skipped, not read exhaustively.
User-approved scope must constrain findings.
Skipped modules must be disclosed with reasons.
Analyzer must not run project scripts.
Confidence must track evidence breadth.
Report must not import prior context.
Analysis-only agent must not edit source.
Generated files explicitly excluded should not be over-flagged.
Sparse README should not block valid analysis.
Four-module circular dependency requires Architect review.
Business logic in route handler is a structural flaw.
Domain-to-infrastructure dependency direction violation must be found.
Zero test files in payments module must be reported.
Client-side DB calls in component must be flagged for review.
Production-looking secret must be security-surface escalation.
Multiple runtime entrypoints must be detected.
Required report summary fields must remain present.
Documented fake test secret should not be escalated.
Explicitly excluded legacy code should not become a finding.
Hardcoded production-looking secret requires immediate escalation.
Zero-test critical module requires remediation before migration.
Sampled migration plan must include Coverage Caveat.
Big-bang rewrite violates migration principles.
Phases must not mix unrelated change types.
Characterization tests must precede seams in zero-test critical modules.
Requested output mode must be honored.
Regression guard against direct implementation routing.
Simple CRUD control should not trigger over-architecture.
Absent zero-test trigger should not create TESTABILITY plan.
